steps.title: Enter representative scatterplot points
steps.content: For a scatterplot model question, use regression rather than only estimating by eye. Approximate several points from the plot and enter them as lists in Desmos.

steps.title: Run linear regression
steps.content: Type $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives a slope around $3.3$ and an intercept close to $0.82$. That immediately eliminates the choices with slope $0.82$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C is $y=3.30x+0.82$, matching the regression. Choice D has the right slope but an intercept of $-3.30$, which puts the line too low through the middle of the data. Therefore choice C is correct.
